moon_deities.name: Nokomis (Cree)
moon_deities.description: Nokomis is the Cree moon grandmother spirit, a wise elder who guides the people and controls the tides.

moon_deities.name: Tecuhtli
moon_deities.description: Tecuhtli is an Aztec moon god associated with nobility, night, and the lunar calendar.

moon_deities.name: Yolkai Estsan
moon_deities.description: Yolkai Estsan is the Navajo goddess of the moon and fertility, associated with the white shell and water.

moon_deities.name: Mapu
moon_deities.description: Mapu is a Mapuche lunar deity representing the earth and moon, central to agricultural cycles.

moon_deities.name: Fati
moon_deities.description: Fati is a Tuvaluan moon god, son of the creator god and associated with night and navigation.

moon_deities.name: Avatea
moon_deities.description: Avatea is a lunar deity of the Cook Islands, associated with light and the duality of day and night.

moon_deities.name: Selu
moon_deities.description: Selu is the Cherokee goddess of corn and the moon, symbolizing sustenance and cycles.

moon_deities.name: Meztli
moon_deities.description: Meztli is another name for the Aztec moon deity, often associated with night, dreams, and time.

moon_deities.name: Chandra-Ma
moon_deities.description: Chandra-Ma is a Buddhist lunar deity representing serenity, meditation, and the passage of time.

moon_deities.name: Phoebe
moon_deities.description: Phoebe is a Titaness associated with the moon and prophecy, grandmother of Apollo and Artemis.

moon_deities.name: Raka
moon_deities.description: Raka is the Maori god of winds with lunar associations, governing tides and weather patterns.

moon_deities.name: Hina-uri
moon_deities.description: Hina-uri is a Hawaiian moon goddess, associated with transformation and the night.

moon_deities.name: Mēn
moon_deities.description: Mēn is a Phrygian moon god depicted with a crescent and associated with time and fate.

moon_deities.name: Khonshu
moon_deities.description: Khonshu is a form of Khonsu, associated with the new moon and protection during travel at night.
